title: Strata #18 — Red Shifting Ground
summary: A horizontal abstract painting from the Strata 2026 series, built around a wide red territorial field crossed by diagonal incised strata. Floating geometric forms create movement, tension and a sense of ground in transformation.
concept: In Strata #18 — Red Shifting Ground, the surface appears as a territory in motion. The diagonal fault lines open the composition, suggesting pressure, displacement and a geological rhythm beneath the visible field. The suspended blocks act as markers within this shifting space, creating a dialogue between structure, instability and silent orientation.
commercial_note: This work belongs to the Strata 2026 series, where abstract territory, fault lines and geometric presences form a visual language of depth and movement. Its horizontal format and intense red palette give the piece a strong panoramic presence, making it suitable for contemporary interiors, collectors and architectural spaces seeking a bold abstract artwork with energy and structure.
